evidence: Top-level decomposition maps cleanly to domain capabilities: src/toolkit/ (framework-agnostic tools), src/plugins/ (lifecycle hooks), src/adapters/ (framework bridges), src/actions/ (executor + pipeline), src/reactive/ (event loop), src/lib/ (shared business logic), cli/ (entry points). A new engineer could explain each directory's purpose.
docs/project-structure.md accurately reflects the actual codebase structure including the toolkit/tools hierarchy, guards, and plugin count. Runtime reality matches documentation.

src/toolkit/index.ts barrel export is well-organized with section comments (Core Types, Session, State Store, Tools, SDK Bridge Types) and explicit named exports — public surface is clear and minimal.
src/index.ts barrel export groups portable modules (declarative engine, source lifecycle, LLM provider, extensions, catalog, observation logger) with descriptive section comments.
src/lib/ has a mixed structure: 8 well-organized subdirectories (auth/, llm/, attestation/, scoring/, sources/, network/, pipeline/, util/) alongside 13 flat files with unclear ownership boundaries.
src/reactive/ subsystem (~1650 LOC across event-loop, event-sources, event-handlers) is largely orphaned — documented as having zero importers — but remains in the production tree.
adapters/ directory cleanly separates eliza/ (9 files, framework bridge) and skill-dojo/ (19 files, SDK operations) — distinct adapter concerns with their own types.ts.
high_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: src/lib/ flat files mix orphaned and active code without clear ownership. reactive/ subsystem persists despite being largely orphaned. These blur the otherwise clean decomposition.

high_level_elegance.dimension_character: The codebase has a strong top-level decomposition with clear domain-aligned directories (toolkit, plugins, adapters, actions, reactive, lib). The public API surfaces via barrel exports are well-organized and documented. The main structural weakness is the src/lib/ flat file zone where 13 files coexist with 8 structured subdirectories, and the orphaned reactive/ subsystem that persists without active consumers. A developer would find the high-level structure intuitive but encounter confusion in the lib/ flat zone.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: Strong domain-aligned directory structure with accurate documentation and clean barrel exports pushes the score up. The orphaned reactive/ subsystem and src/lib/ flat file mess pull it down — a developer cannot tell active from dead code without checking import graphs. Overall this lands at 82.5: solid decomposition with identifiable ownership gaps in specific areas.

issues.summary: src/lib/ mixes 8 organized subdirectories with 13 flat files of unclear ownership
evidence: src/lib/ has 8 subdirectories (auth/, llm/, attestation/, scoring/, sources/, network/, pipeline/, util/) with clear domain alignment, but also 13 flat files including orphaned ones (tips.ts, predictions.ts, mentions.ts, transcript.ts) and active ones (agent-config.ts, spending-policy.ts, budget-tracker.ts). The doc says '33 files remaining flat — ongoing restructuring'.
issues.suggestion: Delete the 4 documented orphaned files. For the remaining 9 flat files, either place them into existing subdirectories by domain affinity (e.g., spending-policy.ts -> scoring/) or create a new subdirectory if they share a theme.

issues.summary: reactive/ subsystem (~1650 LOC) persists in production tree despite being largely orphaned
evidence: Historical review issues document the entire reactive/ subsystem as orphaned with zero importers. event-loop.ts, most event-sources, and all event-handlers have no active consumers. The cli/event-runner.ts entry point exists but the internal event-loop.ts has no importers according to scan evidence.
issues.suggestion: If reactive/ is planned future work, move it to a _staging/ or _future/ directory to signal intent. If abandoned, delete it. Either way, keeping ~1650 LOC of dead code in the production tree undermines the otherwise clean decomposition.

root_causes: Incremental restructuring left src/lib/ in a half-migrated state
Reactive event loop was built speculatively but never fully wired into production

add.description: Top-level directories (toolkit/, plugins/, adapters/, actions/, reactive/, lib/, cli/) each own a clear domain capability. A new engineer can predict directory purpose from the name alone.
docs/project-structure.md and CLAUDE.md accurately reflect the actual directory layout, file counts, and architectural boundaries. This is rare and valuable for developer onboarding.

add.header: Well-organized barrel exports with section comments
add.description: Both src/index.ts and src/toolkit/index.ts use descriptive section comments and explicit named exports, making the public API surface clear and navigable.

The restructuring from flat files to domain subdirectories in src/lib/ is partially done — 8 subdirectories exist but 13 flat files remain. This is a known debt item documented in project-structure.md.
